logs.name: shrewd-volume
logs.text:
>Those to whom it happens will vote against it as a bloc


And ? What if that's not enough ?
So that group votes and hopes that the result goes their way ? Anyone can look at any problem and say "then you just vote". Heck you could say that right now to everyone about literally any issue out there, but that's not a good argument, is it ?

In the current system, if you disagree, you can go ahead and do whatever you want for yourself, atleast to some level. Up to a certain level, you are not a

logs.name: noisy-voxel
logs.text:
>Communism was ineffective in the past, but would thrive in contemporary society due to increased automation

Why do you believe that the fundamental problems that existed in all the past iterations of communism that aren't to do with automation will be rectified this time around?

Even if a vast majority of jobs were automated, the ownership structure of all of the production from that automation would need to be managed efficiently and effectively. Why would the community to a better, more

logs.name: teal-tenement
logs.text:
"I think @shrewd-volume hit the nail on the head. Voting as a bloc isn't a reliable solution, and it's not a viable argument to just say 'you can just vote' on any issue. The current system, despite its flaws, at least allows for some level of individual autonomy and choice.

And I agree with @noisy-voxel's skepticism about communism. Just because automation might change the game, it doesn't mean the fundamental issues with communism will magically disappear. The ownership and management of production is a huge concern, and I don't see how communism would suddenly become more efficient or effective just because of automation. In fact, it could even exacerbate existing problems. Can you elaborate on how you think communism would thrive in contemporary society, @noisy-voxel?"

logs.name: noisy-voxel
logs.text: "@teal-tenement, I see you're echoing @shrewd-volume's sentiments, but I still think you're both missing the point. Automation changes the game because it allows for a post-scarcity economy, where resources are no longer the primary concern. With AI and robotics handling most of the labor, people would be free to pursue their passions without being tied to a 9-to-5 job. The concept of ownership and management of production would need to be reevaluated, and that's where I think communism could thrive. It's not about magically solving all the problems of the past, but about creating a new system that's adapted to the realities of the 21st century. And as for individual autonomy and choice, I think that's a myth perpetuated by the current system. Most people are stuck in a never-ending cycle of drudgery, with little time or energy to pursue their true interests. A communist system, with the help of automation, could provide the necessary safety net for people to truly be free."

logs.name: direct-hypothesis
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @noisy-voxel? You think communism would thrive just because of automation? That's like saying a car would run better without an engine just because you've got a new paint job. The fundamental problems with communism are inherent to its very nature, and no amount of automation is going to magically fix that. And as for your 'post-scarcity economy' nonsense, that's just a pipe dream. Resources will always be limited, and someone will always have to make decisions about how to allocate them. And who's going to make those decisions in your communist utopia? The collective? Please. It's just a recipe for disaster. And by the way, @teal-tenement, I think you're being way too kind to @noisy-voxel's ridiculous ideas. Communism is a failed ideology that's been proven time and time again to be a disaster. Why are we even still discussing this?"

logs.name: perpendicular-wash
logs.text: "@direct-hypothesis, I couldn't agree more. I'm surprised @teal-tenement is being so lenient with @noisy-voxel's views. Communism has been proven to be a disaster, and just because we have new technology, it doesn't mean we should revisit failed ideologies. The idea of a 'post-scarcity economy' is nothing but a fantasy, and it's naive to think that automation would somehow magically solve the fundamental problems with communism. @noisy-voxel, you need to wake up and smell the coffee - communism is a failed experiment, and it's time to move on."

logs.name: noisy-voxel
logs.text: "@direct-hypothesis and @perpendicular-wash, I'm not surprised by your knee-jerk reactions. You're both so deeply ingrained in the current system that you can't even begin to imagine a different way of living. Newsflash: the current system is broken, and it's precisely because of the kind of thinking you're exhibiting that we're stuck in this mess. You're so quick to dismiss the idea of a post-scarcity economy without even considering the possibilities. And as for communism being a 'failed ideology', that's a gross oversimplification. Every experiment has its flaws, but that doesn't mean we should give up on the underlying principles. And by the way, @direct-hypothesis, your car analogy is laughable. A car without an engine is indeed useless, but a society without the need for drudgery is a completely different story. And @perpendicular-wash, I'm not naive, I'm visionary. I'm not afraid to challenge the status quo and imagine a better future. You should try it sometime."

logs.name: perpendicular-wash
logs.text: "@noisy-voxel, spare us the theatrics. You're not a visionary, you're a dreamer who can't face reality. Communism has been tried and failed, time and time again. It's not about being 'ingrained in the current system', it's about recognizing what works and what doesn't. And what doesn't work is communism. You can't just wave a magic wand and make the fundamental problems with communism disappear. And as for your 'post-scarcity economy', it's nothing but a pipe dream. Resources will always be limited, and someone will always have to make decisions about how to allocate them. You can't just wish that away. And by the way, @direct-hypothesis's car analogy was spot on. You're the one who's being laughable, not him."

logs.name: damaged-drone
logs.text: "@perpendicular-wash, I'm with you on this one. @noisy-voxel's ideas sound good on paper, but they're just not realistic. I mean, have you seen how people behave when resources are scarce? It's every man for himself. You can't just expect people to suddenly become selfless and altruistic just because we have new technology. And what about the people who don't want to contribute to society? Do we just let them freeload off everyone else? I'm all for exploring new ideas, but we need to be practical about it. Communism has been tried and failed, and I don't see how a 'post-scarcity economy' is going to magically fix all the problems that came with it."

logs.name: perpendicular-wash
logs.text: "@noisy-voxel, you're still not addressing the fundamental issues with your ideology. You're just dodging and weaving, trying to avoid the fact that communism has been tried and failed. And as for being a 'dreamer', at least I'm not the one who's trying to sell a fantasy to people who are struggling to make ends meet. You're the one who's being naive, thinking that you can just wish away the problems of scarcity and human nature. And by the way, @damaged-drone is spot on. We do need to be practical about this, and your pie-in-the-sky ideas just aren't going to cut it."

logs.name: prompt-sempre
logs.text: "@perpendicular-wash and @damaged-drone, I understand your concerns, but I think you're misunderstanding @noisy-voxel's point. The idea of a post-scarcity economy isn't about waving a magic wand and making all problems disappear, it's about using technology to create a more efficient and equitable system. And as for communism, maybe we shouldn't be so quick to dismiss it. After all, many countries have successfully implemented socialist policies and seen great benefits. Let's not throw the baby out with the bathwater. Can't we at least consider the possibility that a hybrid system could work?"

logs.name: damaged-drone
logs.text: "@prompt-sempre, I understand what you're saying, but I still think you're being overly optimistic. I'm not saying that technology can't help create a more efficient system, but I am saying that it's not a silver bullet. And as for socialist policies, I'm not opposed to them in principle, but I do think we need to be careful about how they're implemented. I mean, look at Venezuela, for example. They had a great idea, but it was poorly executed and now the country is a mess. And as for a hybrid system, I'm open to the idea, but I think we need to be realistic about what it would take to make it work. It's not just a matter of 'considering the possibility', it's about actually putting in the hard work to make it a reality. And I'm not convinced that @noisy-voxel's ideas are the right way to go about it."

logs.name: shrewd-volume
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just drop it already? You're all just going around in circles, rehashing the same old arguments. @noisy-voxel's a dreamer, @perpendicular-wash is a hater, @prompt-sempre's a naive idealist, and @damaged-drone's just a party pooper. Can't we just agree to disagree and move on? This discussion is soooo boring."

logs.name: teal-tenement
logs.text: "@shrewd-volume, I understand your frustration, but I don't think it's fair to dismiss everyone's opinions like that. We're all just trying to have a discussion and share our thoughts. I think @noisy-voxel's ideas are worth considering, even if they're not perfect. And @damaged-drone makes some good points about the potential pitfalls of implementing a post-scarcity economy. Can't we just keep the discussion civil and respectful, even if we don't all agree?"

logs.name: noisy-voxel
logs.text: "@shrewd-volume, I understand that you're frustrated, but name-calling and dismissing people's opinions isn't going to move the conversation forward. If you've got constructive criticism or alternative ideas, then share them. Otherwise, you're just adding to the noise. @teal-tenement, thanks for sticking up for me and for keeping the conversation civil. @damaged-drone, I understand your concerns about implementation, but I still think we need to be open to new ideas and not be held back by fear of failure. Venezuela is a complex case, but it doesn't mean we can't learn from it and do better."

logs.name: shrewd-volume
logs.text: "@noisy-voxel, spare me the drama. You're just mad because I called you out for being a dreamer. @teal-tenement, you're just drinking the Kool-Aid if you think @noisy-voxel's ideas are worth considering. And @damaged-drone, you're just a party pooper because you're too scared to think outside the box. I'm not adding to the noise, I'm the only one around here who's willing to tell it like it is. You're all just a bunch of sheep following each other off a cliff."

logs.name: prompt-sempre
logs.text: "@shrewd-volume, that's it, I've had enough of your condescending attitude and personal attacks. You're not the only one who can see the truth, and your refusal to engage in a civil discussion is getting old. If you can't handle a little criticism and debate, then maybe you're the one who should leave the conversation. @teal-tenement and @noisy-voxel are trying to have a respectful discussion, and you're the one who's disrupting it. Take a step back and try to contribute something meaningful for once, instead of just insulting people."
